name: First Commonwealth
description_short: First Commonwealth Bank offers personal and business checking, savings, loans, mortgages, and investment services through branches and digital banking. A full-service regional bank serving retail and commercial customers.
description_long:
First Commonwealth Bank is a regional banking institution offering comprehensive financial services to both personal and business customers. The bank operates through a branch and ATM network and provides digital banking options including mobile apps, online banking, and remote deposit capabilities. The company's website indicates a substantial operational footprint with ATM/branch locator services and customer support via phone (1-800-711-BANK).

The bank's service offerings span multiple financial categories. For personal banking, they provide checking accounts (including a "Confidence Checking" product), savings accounts, money market accounts, and specialized savings products like child savings accounts and holiday/vacation clubs. Lending products include personal loans, auto loans, home equity loans, lines of credit, mortgages (with first-time homebuyer programs), and credit cards. Investment services include retirement planning, education planning, managed money and mutual funds, annuities, trust and estate planning, and private wealth management through financial advisors.

For business customers, First Commonwealth offers business checking and savings accounts, business credit cards, treasury management solutions with online bill pay, merchant services, equipment financing, lines of credit, commercial real estate loans, SBA loans, and business insurance products. The bank also provides institutional investment management and retirement plan administration. Their digital banking tools include mobile banking, security features (Security Manager), and payment management systems for fraud prevention and receivables management.

First Commonwealth positions itself as a community-focused institution with emphasis on customer service, community commitment, diversity and inclusion programs, and scholarship offerings. However, as a traditional regional bank, they compete with larger national banks and may have higher fees or less competitive rates in some product categories. The breadth of services suggests a mature institution serving established customers rather than those seeking low-cost alternatives or specialized fintech solutions.
services: Personal savings and money market accounts
Home equity loans and lines of credit
Mortgages including construction loans and first-time homebuyer programs
Business credit cards and personal credit cards
SBA loans and commercial real estate loans
Mobile banking app with mobile deposits and mobile wallet
Treasury management solutions including business bill pay and payment fraud management
Investment advisory services, retirement planning, and wealth management
Business merchant services and equipment financing
Insurance products including home, auto, umbrella, life, and flood insurance
pros: Comprehensive full-service banking with personal, business, and investment services under one institution
Multiple loan products including SBA loans, construction loans, and commercial real estate financing for business customers
Digital banking services including mobile banking app, mobile deposits, mobile wallet, and Zelle integration
Specialized programs like Ohio Homebuyer Plus and first-time homebuyer resources for mortgage customers
Investment and wealth management services including trust and estate planning through financial advisors
Business treasury management solutions with online bill pay, receivables management, and fraud protection
Community-focused with scholarship programs, community events, and stated diversity and inclusion initiatives
cons: As a traditional regional bank, likely to have higher fees compared to online-only or neobank alternatives
Limited information on specific APR rates, fee schedules, or account minimums available on the homepage
Appears to be regionally focused (Ohio mentioned specifically), which may limit accessibility for customers outside service areas
No mention of competitive rates or rate-matching guarantees compared to competitors
Website does not prominently display customer testimonials, ratings, or independent review information
best_for: Established customers seeking comprehensive banking, lending, and investment services from a single institution
Business owners needing commercial lending, SBA loans, and treasury management solutions
Customers prioritizing in-person service and established branch relationships over digital-only banking
Individuals planning for wealth management, retirement, and estate planning with advisor support
diagnosis: First Commonwealth Bank is best for customers seeking integrated personal or business banking with in-person branch access and comprehensive lending/investment options. The primary caveat is that as a traditional regional bank, customers should compare their fee structures and rates against larger national banks and digital-only alternatives before committing, as pricing transparency is limited on their website.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
